selected_slice: Introduce minimal recursive constructors in the internal core and explicit xMLF ASTs (`TMu`, `XTMu`, `ERoll`, `EUnroll`) and thread them through existing structural rendering/decoding paths so manually constructed recursive types and terms compile and roundtrip structurally without invoking or changing inference.
why_now: Current master is missing the four milestone-blocking constructors entirely, so M1 cannot progress until the AST shape exists.
This is the smallest slice that matches the roadmap intent: explicit/core recursive-type support that compiles and roundtrips structurally while preserving existing inference behavior.
Landing AST and roundtrip plumbing first creates a safe foundation for later rounds that add parser support, elaboration, or semantics.
acceptance_criteria: Internal core and explicit xMLF AST definitions contain recursive-type and recursive-term constructors corresponding to `TMu`, `XTMu`, `ERoll`, and `EUnroll`.
Existing total traversals, printers/serializers, parsers/decoders, and conversion code that cover neighboring AST nodes are extended so the project builds cleanly with the new constructors.
New regression specs construct representative recursive values directly in the AST and verify structural roundtrip for `mu`, `roll`, and `unroll` forms without exercising inference.
Existing non-recursive behavior remains unchanged, demonstrated by the current test suite continuing to pass after the recursive-AST additions.
out_of_scope: Surface eMLF recursive syntax or user-facing parser changes
Constraint generation, elaboration, inference, or solver semantics for recursive types
Operational/runtime semantics of `roll` and `unroll` beyond structural AST carriage
Public compatibility layers, fallbacks, or convenience APIs

selected_slice: Core-only explicit recursive runtime forms: add ERoll and EUnroll to the elaborated term AST, update exhaustive elaborated-term consumers needed for compilation and runtime semantics, implement Phase 7 typechecking and one-step reduction rules, and land focused fixed regressions in existing Phase 7 specs without pulling public XMLF term syntax into scope.
why_now: M1 is already green and TMu is present on master, so the next lowest unfinished blocker is making recursive types usable in elaborated/runtime terms.
The implementation surface is local and well-bounded: MLF.Types.Elab, MLF.Elab.Types, MLF.Elab.TypeCheck, MLF.Elab.Reduce, plus existing Phase 7 test modules.
This slice unblocks later M3 parser/pretty work while avoiding premature public XMLF term-syntax changes for XRoll and XUnroll.
acceptance_criteria: ElabTerm and ElabTermF gain ERoll ElabType ElabTerm and EUnroll ElabTerm, and all exhaustive elaborated-term consumers compile warning-free after adding conservative cases, including runtime helpers and test-side traversals.

Phase 7 typechecking accepts roll[μa. τ] e only when the annotation is a recursive TMu type and e has the unfolded payload type τ[μa.τ/a], and it accepts unroll e only when e has recursive type μa. τ and returns the same unfolded payload type.
Phase 7 typechecking rejects deterministic error cases for non-recursive roll annotations, non-recursive unroll inputs, and mismatched roll payloads.

Reduction is call-by-value and deterministic for the new forms: ERoll steps its payload until value, ERoll μ v is a value when v is a value, EUnroll steps its scrutinee until value, and EUnroll (ERoll μ v) reduces in one step to v.

Focused regressions are added to existing TypeCheckSpec and ReduceSpec for positive typing, negative typing, and unroll-roll reduction, and existing Phase 7 soundness/progress tests still pass after the constructor fallout updates.
out_of_scope: Public XMLF term constructors, parser, or pretty-printer support for roll and unroll, including XRoll, XUnroll, parseXmlfTerm, prettyXmlfTerm, and src-public/MLF/XMLF.hs changes.
Frontend eMLF surface syntax, normalization, or desugaring for recursive terms or recursive type annotations.
Contractiveness validation, equi-recursive equality, cyclic constraint-graph representation, or any inference/solver changes.
Broad QuickCheck generator expansion to actively synthesize ERoll and EUnroll terms, beyond the minimal helper updates needed to keep existing soundness specs compiling.

selected_slice: Expose recursive types on the public eMLF annotation surface only: add `STMu` to `SrcTy`, parse/pretty/normalize `μa. τ` and `mu a. τ` in frontend annotations, and add a dedicated Phase 1 boundary rejection so normalized `STMu` annotations fail before any recursive lowering into the constraint graph.
why_now: This is the smallest shippable M5 step on current master `0d38bb1b6c88903a44c5b051ab96d57eabb88d23`: the explicit/core layers from M1-M4 already know about recursive types, but the surface frontend still cannot express them. Current code also shows that a shared parser refactor is not required for Attempt 1, while adding `STMu` without an explicit Phase 1 rejection would either fail to compile or accidentally blur the M5/M6 boundary.
acceptance_criteria: `SrcTy` gains `STMu` in both raw and normalized forms, with existing stage/root invariants preserved and compile-through pattern matches updated.
`parseRawEmlfType`/`parseRawEmlfExpr` accept `μa. τ` and `mu a. τ` in source annotations, and `mu` is reserved in the frontend parser.
`prettyEmlfType`/`prettyEmlfExpr` emit canonical `μ` syntax and recursive annotation examples roundtrip through `parse . pretty`.
`normalizeType`/`normalizeExpr` recurse through `STMu` bodies, preserving the recursive wrapper while still normalizing nested alias-bounded `forall` content.

Constraint generation rejects any normalized surface annotation containing `STMu` with a dedicated `ConstraintError`; `inferConstraintGraph`/`runPipelineElab` do not internalize recursive annotations yet.
Focused frontend/public-surface regressions cover the new syntax and the explicit rejection boundary, and existing non-recursive annotation/coercion behavior stays green.
tests_to_run: cabal test mlf2-test --test-show-details=direct --test-options="--match \"Frontend eMLF pretty printer\""
out_of_scope: Lowering `STMu` into constraint-graph nodes or elaborated/core types.
Accepting recursive annotations in `inferConstraintGraph`, `runPipelineElab`, or `runPipelineElabChecked`.
Solver, presolution, reification, runtime, or inference work from M6/M7.
XMLF syntax/runtime changes or any new recursive term forms.
risks: Adding `STMu` touches the indexed `SrcTy` family, so missing one constructor case in syntax/normalize/constraint-generation helpers will break compile or leave partial handling.
Frontend parser precedence and reserved-word changes around `mu` can regress existing surface parsing if they diverge from the already-landed XMLF `μ` behavior.
The rejection path must detect recursive annotations anywhere inside normalized annotation types, including nested `forall` bounds, not just at the outermost node.
`ConstraintError(..)` is publicly re-exported via `MLF.Pipeline`, so a new dedicated constructor changes the public error surface and may require render/spec updates.
notes: Validated the earlier exploratory shape, with one revision: on current master the first slice should avoid a shared `MLF.Parse.Type` refactor and must include a dedicated Phase 1 rejection boundary, because M5 syntax exposure is ready now but M6 graph/pipeline acceptance is still explicitly out of scope.

implementation_summary: Implemented the planned M5 public-surface slice: `SrcTy` now carries `STMu`, frontend parsing accepts both `μa. τ` and `mu a. τ` while reserving `mu`, pretty-printing emits canonical `μ`, normalization preserves recursive wrappers while normalizing nested alias-bounded `forall`s, and Phase 1 now rejects normalized recursive annotations explicitly via `RecursiveAnnotationNotSupported` before any constraint-graph lowering. Added focused parser/pretty/normalize/constraint/public-surface regressions, updated changelog/implementation notes, and verified the full gate green (`1095 examples, 0 failures`). Verification used `/tmp/cabal-config` because the sandbox blocks Cabal’s default `~/.cache/cabal/logs/build.log` build-summary path.

The diff stays within the planned M5 slice: `STMu` is added only to the surface/frontend path, recursive annotations are rejected at the Phase 1 boundary before lowering, the public/runtime/XMLF surfaces outside M5 do not appear to have leaked, and the new focused tests plus the recorded full gate are consistent with that scope.
residual_risks: The defensive `generateConstraintsCore` backstop for recursive core annotations is sensible, but it is only indirectly covered by the current tests; if this seam becomes important later, a direct core-entry regression would make the boundary more explicit.

Frontend type parsing now carries a small amount of local `forall`/`mu` entry logic instead of going entirely through the shared `parseTypeWith` helper. That is acceptable for this slice, but future parser changes will need to keep the frontend and shared type grammar in sync deliberately.
